_doc: One record per shaft (W→E). Reunifies the former pillarDefs / REAL_DEPTH / REAL_FLOOR. Bare scalars drive the model; _prov holds sparse per-field citations/status overriding the record defaults (source=waringo2018, status=documented). modelPos is schematic (crs model-schematic); geo (WGS84) is reserved for surveyed coords.

shafts.notes: Auxiliary debris-filled drop-shaft ~25 m NW of P-5; rubble backfill from 3 m depth, +3 m deeper; dry channel, restored to original state.

geo.note: derived from P-5 (OSM) + documented ~25 m NW offset (p.22); NOT an OSM node. LOW confidence — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 P-7A->P-5A = 24.1 m is consistent with the documented P-5->P-7A ~25 m, but P-7A is a derived auxiliary-branch position, not directly georeferenced. The OSM node 'Quanat -6' lies ~10.7 m from this derived point and has mapsTo=null (unassigned).

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR at the derived coordinate

shafts.notes: Auxiliary shaft ~30 m N of P-4; dry channel at 8 m, 2 m higher than the main channel, so never connected to it; dug in marl, sand-filled.

geo.note: derived from P-5 (OSM) + ~12 m NW (aux channel, vicinity of P-5). LOW confidence — see pd-osm-georef. Auxiliary northern-branch shaft (~30 m N of the main line, fig.5-14 p.22), not directly georeferenced; the OSM node 'Quanat -5' lies ~12 m away and is itself unlabelled (mapsTo=null).

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR at the derived coordinate

shafts.notes: Yellow-sand filled; channel found at 10 m. Site of the 'geological accident' — channel floor breached, water lost into limestone fissures; rebuilt in concrete.

geo.note: OSM node; LOW confidence mapping to P-5 — see pd-osm-georef. P-5's geo is copied from OSM node 'Quanat -5', but the two westernmost OSM nodes ('-6','-5') have no independent label and 'P-5' rests on the unverified '-5'->P-5 assignment. The main line here (P-5, P-4) is entangled with the separate NORTHERN auxiliary branch (P-5A/P-7A, ~25-30 m N, fig.5-14 p.22); no documented main-line inter-shaft distance exists to pin it. fig.3-1 P-5A->P-5 = 14.9 m is within the west-end foreshortening zone. Kept low.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Dry-stone lined (0.8 m wall, 3 concentric rows); base widened to 1.4 m cone; overflow weir diverts water to a lateral channel when the level exceeds 12 cm.
shafts.surfaceState: windowed cover (to view the overflow bifurcation); no concrete surface slab

geo.note: OSM node; MEDIUM confidence mapping to P-4 — see pd-osm-georef. Largest similarity-fit residual (+12.7 m): this is the west-end route bend / plateau-edge transition where the oblique fig.3-1 foreshortening is worst, so the digitised spacing (P-5->P-4 = 12.3 m vs OSM 17.9 m) is unreliable here. LiDAR 366.9 m and the documented main-channel floor 355.4 m corroborate the label; kept medium, not high.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Circular shaft in marl, sand-filled; secured with concrete segments; not used for access.
shafts.surfaceState: concrete-lined shaft body (securing segments), sand-filled — NOT a concrete slab on the surface, so not rendered as a solid cap

geo.note: OSM node; MEDIUM confidence mapping to P-1 — see pd-osm-georef. Both fig.3-1 (P-4->P-1 = 41 m) and OSM (57.4 m) show a large gap before P-1 across the route bend; small similarity-fit residual (+1.9 m) and LiDAR 374.2 m rising smoothly toward the plateau support the label, but the long unbridged leg (no intervening OSM node besides postulated P-2/P-3) keeps this medium.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Marked only with a surface panel; not excavated.

geo.note: OSM node; MEDIUM confidence mapping to P0 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 (P-1->P0 = 14.0 m) and OSM (19.1 m) agree in shape; small fit residual (-1.4 m); LiDAR 379.4 m on the rising hillside is consistent. Kept medium (not high) because P0 is 'marked only, not excavated' (no depth/floor to cross-check) and sits in the tight P-1/P0/P1 cluster whose absolute spacing is hard to separate on the oblique figure.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

dates.note: not excavated — marked only with a surface panel

shafts.notes: At the bend in the route; likely the first shaft sunk; 6 m below the plateau edge in a natural depression. Total depth 30 m; an intermediate 'sounding' gallery at 20 m has no channel.

geo.note: OSM node; MEDIUM confidence mapping to P1 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 (P0->P1 = 11.3 m) and OSM (15.6 m) agree; small fit residual (-4.2 m); LiDAR 386.1 m fits the rise onto the plateau. Kept medium because P1 anchors the P1->P2 leg that is internally inconsistent (see P2, low) — the OSM P1 vs P2 spacing contradicts the brochure's 'P2 unusually close to P1' (p.16-17).

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Western edge of the Pëtschend plateau; still backfilled in its original state; unusually close to P1 for survey reasons.

geo.note: OSM node; LOW confidence mapping to P2 — see pd-osm-georef. The OSM 'P2' node conflicts with the documented geometry: OSM P1->P2 = 23.5 m while OSM P2->P3 = 19.8 m vs documented P2-P3 = 28 m (fig.3-1 digitised P2->P3 = 24.4 m). The brochure states P2 is 'unusually close to P1' (p.17), but the OSM node makes P1->P2 the LONGER leg. P2 is the largest negative residual (-11 m) in the OSM->fig-3-1 similarity fit: the OSM 'P2' node reads ~one-third too far east along the route. Coordinate NOT re-mapped (no unambiguous alternative), only flagged.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Exactly on the P1–P5 line, 28 m from P2; crosses the plateau at its narrowest to minimise >30 m shafts; still in original state.

geo.note: OSM node; HIGH confidence mapping to P3 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 digitised P2->P3 = 24.4 m matches documented 28 m within oblique-figure tolerance; small similarity-fit residual (-4.5 m); LiDAR 392.5 m sits on the flat Petschend plateau top (fig.4-2), consistent with neighbours P4/P5. Sequence unambiguous.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Half-way between P3 and P5; mouth first excavated with P5; concrete rings + slab cap; base later tunnelled through to link P5→P3.
shafts.surfaceState: concrete slab cap (concrete rings + slab cap on the surface)

surfaceCase.note: The ONLY shaft with a documented CONCRETE slab on the present-day surface (concrete rings + slab cap) — rendered as a solid cap. Every other excavated shaft head carries a modern METAL lid (site-surface-state), not concrete, so it renders as a mesh.

geo.note: OSM node; HIGH confidence mapping to P4 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 digitised P3->P4 = 32.2 m agrees with OSM 35.7 m; small similarity-fit residual (-4.6 m); LiDAR 391.7 m on the flat plateau top (fig.4-2), consistent with P3/P5.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Deepest shaft; eastern plateau edge; first cleared to base (3 Oct 1986). Diameter 1.2 m, narrowing to 0.85 m at torus bulges; the channel passes tangentially.
shafts.surfaceState: windowed metal cover (to view the ~35 m depth); no concrete surface slab

depthM.note: shaft quoted 36 m; 35 m measured to gallery base

geo.note: OSM node; HIGH confidence mapping to P5 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 digitised P4->P5 = 32.0 m matches OSM 31.7 m almost exactly; small similarity-fit residual (-1.2 m); LiDAR 392.1 m is the eastern plateau edge (deepest shaft, 36 m; fig.4-2) immediately before the Haedchen drop.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

dates.note: cleared ~10 m in 1913; base reached 3 Oct 1986 — the gallery was rediscovered here

shafts.notes: First Haedchen shaft surveyed from below ground. A 1.2 m stepped fall in the gallery at its base is a provision for level adjustment; debris/sinter cone with iron oxide. Rock bed ~10 m under ~10 m of soft ground.

geo.note: OSM node; HIGH confidence mapping to P6 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 digitised P5->P6 = 23.5 m matches OSM 23.7 m; LiDAR 382.1 m captures the sharp plateau-edge->Haedchen drop (-10 m from P5) exactly as fig.4-2 shows P6 as the first Haedchen shaft. Note: far/east end of fig.3-1, so digitised absolute spacing is the least reliable here, but the OSM-digitised agreement is excellent.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Located from below ground along the eastbound gallery; cleared with heavy machinery; counter-excavation between P7 and P8.

geo.note: OSM node; HIGH confidence mapping to P7 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 digitised P6->P7 = 16.4 m equals OSM 16.4 m; LiDAR 378.2 m in the Haedchen depression, consistent with P8/P9 (fig.4-2). Far-east end of the oblique figure (foreshortened), but OSM-digitised agreement is essentially exact.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Cleared normally; a spring influx ~25 m³/d enters 2 m upstream on the left. An oak beam dated to 114 AD (felling ~131 AD) was found near its base.

geo.note: OSM node; HIGH confidence mapping to P8 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 digitised P7->P8 = 20.1 m matches OSM 20.5 m; LiDAR 377.1 m in the Haedchen depression, consistent with P7/P9 (fig.4-2). Far-east end of the oblique figure, but OSM-digitised agreement is excellent.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

dates.note: eastern campaign; oak beam (114 AD) found near its base by 1991

shafts.notes: The 'mother shaft' — most upstream/easternmost. The gallery ends 6 m east of P9; there is no channel for the first ~13 m. A portal niche with a seepage gutter; oak shovels were found in the mud nearby.

geo.note: OSM node; HIGH confidence mapping to P9 — see pd-osm-georef. fig.3-1 digitised P8->P9 = 21.3 m matches OSM 22.3 m; LiDAR 377.2 m in the Haedchen depression (mother shaft, easternmost), consistent with P7/P8 (fig.4-2). Far-east end of the oblique figure, but OSM-digitised agreement is excellent.

surfaceElevM.note: 0.5 m LiDAR sampled at the OSM coordinate

shafts.notes: Not yet found. Existence postulated from the qanat shaft-spacing regularity west of P-4.
Not yet found. Existence postulated from shaft-spacing regularity west of P-4.
Not yet located; existence assumed from the spacing between P-5A and P-7A.
